Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Additively Manufacturable High-Strength Aluminum Alloys with Coarsening-Resistant Microstructures Achieved via Rapid Solidification

Composition_Info.Note: Designation also reported as Al-2.33Er-3.19Zr-2.73Ni wt.%

Process_Text.original: LPBF (laser powder bed fusion) additive manufacturing; powder manufactured by ultrasonic atomization; process parameters (power and scan speed) optimized for highest density; crack-free macroscale specimens fabricated (28x6x6 mm3 and 6x6x6 mm3). Build direction specified in EBSD images.
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF additive manufacturing from ultrasonic atomized powder; optimized LPBF parameters for crack-free dense specimens

Process_Info.Equipment: LPBF system (DMG MORI LT30 machine as per acknowledgements)

Microstructure_Text.original: Non-textured equiaxed grains observed via EBSD from top and side of printed cube. Melt pool boundaries visible at low magnification. Primary Al3Ni phase uniformly distributed; one large Zr-rich precipitate observed. Grain boundary precipitates (average major axis 104 nm, std dev 114 nm, average area 3.87 um2) identified as metastable ternary Al23Ni6M4 phases enriched in Er and Ni. L12 nanoprecipitates (1-5 nm) identified inside grain interiors via STEM-HAADF with {001}-type superlattice reflections. Ordered nanoclusters with average radius 0.70 nm and area fraction 17%. Core-shell structure for Er and Zr distribution in L12 phases confirmed.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Non-textured equiaxed grains; L12 nanoprecipitates (1-5 nm) with core-shell Er/Zr structure; metastable ternary phases at grain boundaries (~104 nm); 17% area fraction of ordered L12 nanoclusters

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Room temperature; as-built condition; ductility of 19% with 25% sacrifice in as-built strength (from 372 to 279 MPa)
Properties_Info.Note: As-built ductility; strength sacrifice refers to aged condition

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: As-built condition; parallel to build direction and on top of samples

Composition_Info.Note: Designation also reported as Al-2.33Er-3.19Zr-2.73Ni wt.%; peak-aged condition at 400C for 8h

Process_Info.Process_Category: Laser Powder Bed Fusion (LPBF) + isothermal aging

Process_Text.original: LPBF (laser powder bed fusion) additive manufacturing; powder manufactured by ultrasonic atomization; process parameters optimized for highest density; crack-free specimens. Subsequent isothermal aging at 400C for 8 h (peak hardness condition).
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF additive manufacturing followed by aging at 400C for 8h

Process_Info.Equipment: LPBF system (DMG MORI LT30 machine as per acknowledgements)

Microstructure_Text.original: L12 nanoprecipitates (1-5 nm) with superlattice reflections confirmed by STEM-HAADF. Ordered nanoclusters (average radius 0.70 nm, area fraction 17%) with L12-type atomic order identified via Getis-Ord Gi* statistic. Core-shell Er/Zr distribution in L12 phases confirmed by atomic-resolution EDS. APT confirms L12-Al3(Er,Zr) precipitation corresponding to 1-5 nm L12 precipitates. Ternary Al23Ni6M4 phase confirmed by APT composition near predicted stoichiometry. Grain boundary precipitates: metastable ternary phases (Er, Ni enriched).
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Peak-aged microstructure with L12 nanoprecipitates (1-5 nm); 17% area fraction of ordered L12 nanoclusters; core-shell Er/Zr structure; ternary Al23Ni6M4 phase at grain boundaries

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Room temperature; aged condition (25% sacrifice from as-built strength of 372 MPa)
Properties_Info.Note: Aged condition yield strength; as-built yield strength ~372 MPa

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Room temperature; aged 8 h at 400C (peak condition)
Properties_Info.Note: 50% higher than Gong et al. benchmark printable Al alloy; 5-fold higher than equivalent cast alloy
Estimated from Figure 6c stress-strain curve for peak-aged condition

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Peak hardness after 8-10 h aging at 400C; ~50% higher than AM benchmark alloy (133 HV)
Properties_Info.Note: Higher than wrought Al 7075 under identical aging conditions

Composition_Info.Note: Designation also reported as Al-2.33Er-3.19Zr-2.73Ni wt.%; over-aged condition at 400C for 48h

Process_Info.Process_Category: Laser Powder Bed Fusion (LPBF) + isothermal aging

Process_Text.original: LPBF additive manufacturing; powder manufactured by ultrasonic atomization. Subsequent isothermal aging at 400C for 48 h (over-aged condition).
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF additive manufacturing followed by aging at 400C for 48h (over-aged)

Process_Info.Equipment: LPBF system (DMG MORI LT30 machine as per acknowledgements)

Microstructure_Text.original: Similar gradual decrease in hardness as benchmark alloy after prolonged aging. Ternary phase sizes remain in 1-4 um range. L12 phases inside grain interiors remain in 1-5 nm range. Strength maintained without significant decrease after 48 h aging at 400C.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Over-aged at 400C for 48h; hardness maintained without significant decrease; L12 nanoprecipitates (1-5 nm) and ternary phases (1-4 um) retained

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: After 48 h aging at 400C; gradual decrease from peak hardness of 200 HV
Properties_Info.Note: Hardness maintained without significant decrease compared to peak (200 HV after 8h)

Composition_Info.Note: Control alloy with identical molar percentage of Er and Zr as ML-optimized alloy but without Ni; L12 phases form directly during rapid solidification without metastable ternary phase

Process_Text.original: Induction melted and laser scanned to mimic AM solidification conditions. L12 phases directly form during rapid solidification (no ternary phase as reactant reservoir).
Process_Text.simplified: Induction melting + laser scanning (rapid solidification); control without Ni to demonstrate role of metastable ternary phase

Microstructure_Text.original: SEM-EDS shows several large clusters of micrometer-scale Zr-rich phases within grain interiors. These Zr-rich clusters are not present in the ML-optimized alloy with Ni. L12 phases form directly during rapid solidification without the metastable ternary phase as intermediate.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Micrometer-scale Zr-rich clusters in grain interiors; no metastable ternary phase due to absence of Ni

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: As-built/laser-scanned condition; 34% lower than ML-optimized composition with Ni (147 HV after aging)
Properties_Info.Note: Without Ni ternary phase, no submicron metastable phases form; demonstrates necessity of Ni for nanoscale precipitation

Composition_Info.Note: Benchmark printable Al alloy from Gong et al. (PCT/US2018/062761); prepared by same induction melting + laser scanning method; reported hardness 100 HV after aging at 400C for 8h

Process_Text.original: Prepared by induction melting and laser scanning (same method as control alloys) to mimic AM solidification conditions. Used as benchmark for comparison.
Process_Text.simplified: Induction melting + laser scanning (mimicking AM); benchmark from Gong et al.

Microstructure_Text.original: Benchmark printable Al alloy prepared by the same method (induction melting + laser scanning). Has lower hardness (100 HV after aging at 400C for 8h) compared to ML-optimized alloy (147 HV). Has higher coarsening metric and lower L12 phase content than ML-optimized design.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Benchmark printable Al alloy; lower L12 content and higher coarsening metric than ML-optimized design

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Room temperature; aged condition from Gong et al. benchmark printable Al alloy
Properties_Info.Note: ML-optimized alloy has ~50% higher yield strength (395 MPa peak UTS)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: After aging at 400C for 8h; induction melted + laser scanned condition
Properties_Info.Note: ML-optimized alloy has 47% higher hardness (147 HV); AM benchmark has 133 HV after LPBF + aging

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: After LPBF + aging at 400C for 8-10h; AM benchmark alloy
Properties_Info.Note: ML-optimized LPBF alloy has 50% higher hardness (200 HV) than this AM benchmark

Composition_Info.Note: Al-Ce-Mn AM alloy from Plotkowski et al.; exploited both precipitation hardening and laminated eutectic phase strengthening; strength lower than ML-optimized design which uses precipitation hardening alone; Al20Mn2Ce phase transforms to Al11Ce3 and Al12Mn after aging

Process_Text.original: Al-Ce-Mn alloy fabricated by additive manufacturing (LPBF or DED); from Plotkowski et al. study. Used as benchmark comparison. Exploits both precipitation strengthening and laminated eutectic phases for strengthening.
Process_Text.simplified: Al-Ce-Mn AM alloy benchmark from Plotkowski et al.

Microstructure_Text.original: Al-Ce-Mn alloy with Al6Mn phase increasing as-built hardness; transforms to Al11Ce3 and Al12Mn after aging. Exploits both precipitation strengthening and laminated eutectic phase strengthening (two strengthening mechanisms). Despite using two mechanisms, the ML-optimized Al-Er-Zr-Y-Yb-Ni alloy with only precipitation hardening achieves higher strength.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Al-Ce-Mn AM alloy with multi-phase precipitation; uses two strengthening mechanisms (precipitation + eutectic)
